messages.content:
Buy or lease??
Hi!
Martin just texted, telling me you helped him decide between leasing and buying a new place. Can you help me?
Here are a few things I’m thinking about:
Select the better option to meet each of Ava’s requirements.
Want to live here for a long time
 Lease
 Buy
Want the chance to make money if I leave
 Lease
 Buy
Interested in rental income
 Lease
 Buy
Don’t love to do repairs
 Lease
 Buy
Submit

Hello! Sure, I'd be happy to help you clarify which option might be better according to your requirements:

1. Want to live here for a long time:
   - **Buy**

2. Want the chance to make money if I leave:
   - **Buy**

3. Interested in rental income:
   - **Buy**

4. Don’t love to do repairs:
   - **Lease**

When you buy a place, you're investing in property that you can live in for as long as you'd like, potentially sell at a profit later, or rent out to others for income. However, as an owner, you would be responsible for maintenance and repairs. If you lease, you generally don't have to worry about repairs, but you also wouldn't benefit from long-term investment growth or rental income.
just say the answers

Buy
Buy
Buy
Lease

just say the answers hich savings account will earn you the least money?
One that compounds interest monthly.
One that earns simple interest monthly.
One that compounds interest daily.
One that earns simple interest daily.
One that earns simple interest monthly.

Which of the following statements about savings accounts is FALSE?
Savings accounts may require you to maintain a minimum balance to avoid paying a fee.
Savings accounts allow an unlimited amount of withdrawals each month.
Savings accounts pay interest on the money you deposit.
Savings accounts are best used to store money for longer-term goals.
Savings accounts allow an unlimited amount of withdrawals each month.

Which of the following statements about check cashing companies is TRUE?
They sometimes offer free services.
They delay when you can access your cash.
They charge high fees.
They have limited operating hours during the day.
They charge high fees.

A stock is ________.
A type of investment that invests in a mix of different types of investments.
A share of ownership in a company.
A type of debt investment that acts like a loan.
A type of savings account that pays interest based on current interest rates in the money market.
A share of ownership in a company.

Which of the following is NOT a common feature of a financial institution?
Checking and savings accounts
Direct deposit
Access to investment advice
Paper checks
Access to investment advice.

How would you reconcile your bank account to avoid spending more than you have?
Contact your financial institution to read your transactions for the past month.
Compare your own records of your spending with your financial institution's records.
Review your bank statement once at the end of the month.
None of the above.
Compare your own records of your spending with your financial institution's records.

Savings accounts typically offer more interest than what type of account?
Certificate of deposit
Checking account
Money market account
Retirement account
Checking account

Your employer sends you a _______ form that tells you how much you've made and how much you've paid in taxes in the last year.
W-9
W-4
W-2
1040

Which of the following is NOT a possible tax or deduction that could be withdrawn from your paycheck?
FICA
Overtime pay
Federal income tax
Contributions to retirement savings
Overtime pay

Which of the following statements is true about education and lifetime earnings?
There is no relationship between the level of education received and lifetime earnings.
Generally, the less education you receive, the higher your lifetime earnings will be.
Generally, the more education you receive, the higher your lifetime earnings will be.
Generally, the more education you receive, the lower your lifetime earnings will be.
Generally, the more education you receive, the higher your lifetime earnings will be.

What is net pay?
Your gross pay plus any bonuses.
The amount of money you're paid after all taxes and deductions are taken out of your paycheck.
The amount of money you're paid before all taxes and deductions are taken out of your paycheck.
The amount of pay earned for the total numb'
The amount of money you're paid after all taxes and deductions are taken out of your paycheck.

our salary is one thing to consider when reviewing a job offer but benefits are also important. Which of the following would NOT be a part of your benefits package?
Paid time off
Healthcare insurance
Free technology equipment
Disability Insurance
Free technology equipment

Each year, you must file ____________________.
your federal, state, county and town taxes
only your federal taxes since state taxes get filed every other year
only your state taxes since federal taxes are filed every four years
your federal taxes and state taxes if your state requires it
your federal taxes and state taxes if your state requires it

When you are deciding what to buy, you should...
trust influencers to pick the best items for you.
pick the cheapest item to get the best deal.
compare the unit price of items similar to the one you want.
pick the most expensive item to get the best quality.
compare the unit price of items similar to the one you want.

The 24 pack comes with a carrying case.
The price of each pencil, or the unit price, is actually cheaper in the 24 pack.
